description: NPM Audit Baseline - Accepted dev-only vulnerabilities as of 2026-06-15

acceptedFindings.title: esbuild: Missing binary integrity verification in Deno module enables remote code execution via NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY
acceptedFindings.reasoning: esbuild is a build-time tool only. The vulnerability affects the binary download integrity check during `npm ci`, but the packaged Electron application contains only the built output (JavaScript/CSS), not esbuild itself. Zero runtime exposure for end users.
acceptedFindings.requirements: Attacker must control npm registry OR set NPM_CONFIG_REGISTRY env var
acceptedFindings.planToUpgrade: Blocked by electron-vite support - vite 8.x uses rolldown instead of esbuild. Revisit when electron-vite releases v6.x+ with vite 8.x support.

acceptedFindings.title: esbuild allows arbitrary file read when running the development server on Windows
acceptedFindings.reasoning: Only affects esbuild serve() API on Windows during `npm run dev`. The packaged Electron application does not include the dev server. Zero runtime exposure for end users. Risk limited to Windows developers in development environment.
acceptedFindings.requirements: Developer on Windows running dev server + attacker crafts specific request
acceptedFindings.planToUpgrade: Same as GHSA-gv7w-rqvm-qjhr - blocked by electron-vite v6.x

riskAssessment.runtimeExposure: NONE - All vulnerable packages are build/dev tools only
riskAssessment.shippedCodeExposure: NONE - Packaged Electron app contains only built output
riskAssessment.developerImpact: LOW - Requires compromised npm registry or Windows dev environment
riskAssessment.buildIntegrityRisk: MEDIUM - Developers running npm ci could get poisoned build tools

nextSteps: Monitor electron-vite releases for v6.x with vite 8.x support
When electron-vite v6.x is available, upgrade: vite->8.0.16+, electron-vite->6.0.0+, @vitejs/plugin-react->6.0.2+
Verify all three CI checks pass: `ci`, `build-linux`, `build-macos`
Update this baseline to reflect the upgrade
